title: ugetregs
text: ugetregs Get the string value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). Explain this code long ugetregs (shortnum, unsigned char*buffer, shortmax); Copy Parameters num Number of the Uniface general variable. Allowed values are 1 through 99. buffer Contents of the general variable (Output). max Maximum size of the string data to be retrieved. Because the buffer must also hold the zero terminator, the length of the buffer pointed to by buffer must be at least max +1 bytes. The maximum value you can specify here is 32,766. Return Values The number of bytes copied into buffer , not including the zero terminator. Zero is returned if the value of num is out of range. Use Library: yrtl Description ugetregs copies a maximum of max bytes of string data from the specified general variable num to buffer , adding a zero terminator after the last byte. The zero terminator is always written, even if you did not copy all of the contents of the general register. Trailing spaces are not removed. The length of buffer should be at least max +1 bytes. ugetregs can transfer a maximum of 32,767 bytes in total, even if you put a higher value in the max parameter. If you need to transfer more data, you should consider transferring the data from a field, instead of from a general variable, using ufget in combination with ufgetc . If the data in the variable contains information that is not in Font 0, ‘shift font’ characters appear in the string. Profile and subfield characters, if present in a field, are also returned in the buffer, so you should be prepared to handle these. For information about the representation of these characters, refer to the "Subfield Separator and Profile Characters" topic. ProcScript The following example retrieves and prints the string value of general register $10 : Explain this code $10 = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" perform "GETREG10" $10 = "12345 " perform "GETREG10" Copy 3GL code Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void GETREG10 () { char value[21]; long len; len = ugetregs(10, value, 20); printf("Length = %d, Value is : '%s'\n", len, value); } Copy The following example prints out these lines: Explain this code Length = 20, Value is : 'abcdefghijklmnopqrst' Length = 6, Value is : '12345 ' Copy Related Links Subfield Separator and Profile Characters Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters. ugetreg Get the integer value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). ugetregf Get the double-precision value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). uputreg Copy a long integer value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99).
